title: Avatar: Fire and Ash Fourth Weekend Box Office Report
text: Avatar: Fire and Ash secured its fourth consecutive #1 position at the domestic box office during its fourth weekend in theaters. The science fiction epic earned approximately $21.3 million over the three-day period, bringing its cumulative domestic total to $342.6 million. Two major new releases debuted this weekend but failed to dethrone the James Cameron film. Paramount's killer chimpanzee thriller Primate earned roughly $11.3 million, placing it in second position. Gerard Butler's post-apocalyptic film Greenland 2: Migration underperformed with an $8 million debut, while the Sydney Sweeney thriller The Housemaid remained competitive with $11.1 million. The article notes that it remains to be seen if next weekend's debuting title, the horror sequel 28 Years Later: The Bone Temple can do so. Given that the original film debuted with $30 million domestically, Avatar's dominance could face a significant challenge in the coming weeks. However, analysts suggest the James Cameron production might recover to #1 in its sixth weekend if the horror sequel experiences typical week-two drops. Fire and Ash follows the pattern of its predecessors, with both 2009's Avatar and 2022's The Way of Water maintaining the top position for seven consecutive weekends.
